market_context: Constructive semis tape: 'SK Hynix 7X Oversubscription Boosts Semi Stocks; Earnings Season Ahead; Oil Pulls Back After Iran Attacks' (07-09) and 'Semiconductor ETFs Draw Record $7.1 Billion as Investors Buy the AI Chip Dip' (07-10); 'ETF Inflows Hit Record $1 Trillion In H1' (07-08). VOO 690.66, +0.1% over 30d. Data pull PARTIAL for the 7th consecutive run: fundamentals failed for all 7 tickers (proxy 403), prices+news intact — sector caps remain unenforceable, so any buy would be rejected sector-unknown. Portfolio is 100% cash; the holdings-only breaking-news web sweep was not applicable. Every ticker's headline list was non-empty today. Earnings season imminent adds event risk; all holds.

signals.thesis: Second-order tape only: 'Record Companies Push to Label AI Songs on Streaming Platforms' (WSJ via Benzinga, 07-10) touches Apple Music but is not a revenue event, and a Khosla-backed startup ran the largest-ever AI model on an iPhone (07-09) — directional positive, unquantified. Price 316.16 sits AT the 30d high (+1.7% 30d) with no fresh fundamental catalyst. Falsified toward buy if on-device AI shows up in guidance; toward sell if DMA remedies hit Services.

signals.thesis: Regulatory watch item: UK designated Amazon, Google, Microsoft and Oracle cloud services as critical third parties (07-10) — oversight burden, not yet a P&L event. Altman's 'rise in cost of compute, memory definitely a headwind' (07-09) cuts against the whole AI-capex complex, Azure included. Price 387.13, -6.9% over 30d, lower third of range. Still waiting on Azure guidance before acting either way. Falsified toward sell on a soft Azure print; toward buy if margin guidance rises.
